title: How is the heatwave affecting the North West of England?

input.lead: An amber warning of extreme heat is expected to affect things from travel to bin collections
input.summary: An amber warning of extreme heat is expected to affect things from travel to bin collections
input.evidence_text: An amber warning of extreme heat in the North West has been issued by the Met Office while some other parts of England and Wales have a rare red warning for extreme heat The Met Office says hot and humid weather is expected to continue until Thursday in the region, with exceptionally warm temperatures both day and night Here is a look at the impacts the heatwave is having on North West England There is no legal maximum temperature for classrooms – and the government does not normally advise schools to close Many classrooms do not have air conditioning and are too hot to use but schools are encouraged to stay open if possible and to make adjustments to help children feel cooler and more comfortable The adjustments include relaxing uniform requirements, with some allowing children to wear PE kits, ventilating classrooms and using fans if temperatures are below 35C. Above 35C, fans can worsen dehydration

title: Mum fears deaf son is missing out at school due to BSL gap

input.lead: Caroline says her 10-year-old son Thomas is left isolated due to a lack of BSL qualified staff
input.summary: Caroline says her 10-year-old son Thomas is left isolated due to a lack of BSL qualified staff
input.evidence_text: The mother of a 10-year-old boy who is profoundly deaf fears her son is missing out on his education due to a lack of teaching staff with British Sign Language (BSL) Caroline, from Blackley in Manchester, believes the current situation is leaving her son Thomas - who is also visually impaired - isolated and she is concerned he is "not learning anything meaningful" "Many people assume all special schools can meet every disabled child's needs, but really when your child has a multi-sensory impairment (MSI) it's like a postcode lottery," she said A statement from Manchester City Council, on behalf of the school, said it worked to ensure the "best offer and outcomes for all of our children" A Freedom of Information request by the charity Sense found 65% of local authorities in the north-west of England did not employ a single specialist teacher to support deafblind children Deafblindness is defined by the Deadfblind UK charity as "the loss of sight and hearing to the point where your communication, mobility and ability to access information are impacted"

title: TV Festival to move from Edinburgh to Manchester after 50 years

input.lead: The event is relocating from the Scottish capital next year in a move to "radically" cut costs
input.summary: The event is relocating from the Scottish capital next year in a move to "radically" cut costs
input.evidence_text: The TV Festival is relocating from Edinburgh to Manchester next year after half a century Organisers said it was moving from the Scottish capital "amid increasing challenges around accessibility, affordability and sustainability" across the television industry The TV Foundation, which has run the event since 1976, praised Greater Manchester's vision for the festival which "combined genuine creative ambition and future-facing energy" It said it would be able to "radically reduce" costs for delegates by moving the festival to Manchester. Edinburgh will host the event for the last time on 25-28 August The festival features independent debate within the television industry, with more than 60 keynotes, debates, and masterclasses It attracts some of the biggest names in the sector, with presenter Graham Norton, actor Michael Sheen and American comedian, writer and producer Tina Fey taking part in last year's event

title: Greater Manchester's mayoral election explained

input.lead: Voters in all 10 boroughs will elect a new mayor to replace Andy Burnham on 30 July
input.summary: Voters in all 10 boroughs will elect a new mayor to replace Andy Burnham on 30 July
input.evidence_text: People living in Greater Manchester will head to the polls in July to vote for who they think should be the region's mayor Residents living in all 10 boroughs will vote in the Greater Manchester mayoral by-election on 30 July We take a look at why it is happening, what the timetable is, and how the supplementary vote system works The lection was triggered when Andy Burnham, who held the office for nine years, was elected the MP for Makerfield in last Thursday's parliamentary by-election By law, an MP cannot sit in the House of Commons and hold a metropolitan mayor role that has powers over local policing The Greater Manchester mayoral contest has to take place within 35 days of the post becoming vacant

title: Friends open World Cup fan zone at Manchester's Victoria Baths

input.lead: The organisers of the events in Manchester are gearing up for England's second game on Tuesday
input.summary: The organisers of the events in Manchester are gearing up for England's second game on Tuesday
input.evidence_text: Three friends have opened a World Cup fan zone, despite never having organised an event before After experiencing packed fan zones during previous matches, Jack Nunec, 29, Jon Beer, 24, and Lewis Atherton, 27, talked about what it would be like to host their own The friends never thought their concept would "snowball into an actual event", but after months of planning the three mates have opened a World Cup fan zone at Manchester's Victoria Baths "It's easy to dream about the concept of a fan zone [but] it was a bit of a semi-fantasy," Jack told BBC Radio Manchester ahead of England's second game of the tournament. "We are so overwhelmed it has become a reality." Jack, from Skelmersdale, Jon, from Exeter, and Lewis, from New Zealand, met at a football club in Manchester Although Jack works in the legal department of an events company, none of them had hosted an event before

title: Labour announces Manchester council boss as metro mayor candidate

input.lead: Bev Craig is Labour's candidate for metropolitan mayor of Greater Manchester
input.summary: Bev Craig is Labour's candidate for metropolitan mayor of Greater Manchester
input.evidence_text: The leader of Manchester City Council will be Labour's candidate to succeed Andy Burnham as mayor of Greater Manchester As well as leading the city council, Bev Craig is the deputy mayor for economy, business and inclusive growth on the region's combined authority She said: "Greater Manchester is a special place – from the Industrial Revolution, the trade union and co-operative movements and the suffragettes, this place has always fought for progress "This place changed my life and I owe it everything. It gave me opportunities I could never have imagined, and I've spent my career trying to give something back." The by-election for a new metropolitan mayor was called after Burnham was elected as MP for Makerfield in last Thursday's by-election Other candidates who have confirmed their intention to stand in the contest – set to be held on July 30 – include, in alphabetical order

title: Bury woman murdered her husband, 84, who had dementia

input.lead: Daryl Berman is found guilty despite claiming her husband had accidentally sustained his injuries
input.summary: Daryl Berman is found guilty despite claiming her husband had accidentally sustained his injuries
input.evidence_text: A woman murdered her 84-year-old husband who had dementia and then tried to cover up her crime by claiming the fatal stab wound was accidental Minshull Street Crown Court in Manchester heard that Daryl Berman, 72, stabbed her husband of 27 years in the chest at their home in Bury on 13 March She had claimed David Berman's injuries had been sustained as he fell carrying a tray with a knife into the kitchen, but on Tuesday the jury found her guilty of murder After the case, senior crown prosecutor Sazeeda Ismail said: "The jury saw through her lies and convicted her of murder, due to the strength of the prosecution's case." Berman had "lied to paramedics, police and family members when she claimed Mr Berman had accidentally sustained his fatal injuries", she added She will be sentenced at the same court on 3 July

title: Manchester firm bombarded people in debt with 5.5m texts

input.lead: KRA Consultancy Ltd has been fined £300k for sending more than 5.5m nuisance texts
input.summary: KRA Consultancy Ltd has been fined £300k for sending more than 5.5m nuisance texts
input.evidence_text: A business has been fined £300,000 for bombarding people in debt with more than 5.5 million unlawful texts, including fake bailiff threats Manchester-based KRA Consultancy sent messages appearing to come from bailiffs to people's phones to frighten them into responding, the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) said The firm was found to have sent 5,575,715 unsolicited direct marketing texts between April 2022 and May 2025, promoting debt solutions to people who had already been turned down for loans Andy Curry, the ICO's head of investigations, said it was a "calculated, unlawful scheme, and it caused real fear and distress to people who were already struggling with debt" People in financial difficulty deserved support, not exploitation, Curry said "KRA deliberately sought these people out - knowing they might be especially susceptible to this kind of high-pressure marketing - and bombarded them with illegal texts," said Curry

title: Nurse exposed by undercover BBC Panorama reporter struck off

input.lead: Emma Doherty was recorded saying on patient "needed a thrashing"
input.summary: Emma Doherty was recorded saying on patient "needed a thrashing"
input.evidence_text: A senior nurse recorded saying that an autistic patient needed "a good thrashing" has been struck off Emma Doherty was a Band 6 deputy ward manager at the Edenfield Centre - part of Prestwich Hospital - which was designed to care for patients with severe psychiatric illnesses who had been detained for their own safety In September 2022 the BBC aired Panorama: Undercover Hospitals: Patients at Risk which included covert filming from inside the unit, run by Greater Manchester Mental Health (GMMH) NHS Foundation Trust At the time the film was broadcast GMMH said it had taken "immediate action" to protect patients as soon as it was made aware The Edenfield Centre was temporarily closed after Panorama's film and has since been re-branded the Riverside Centre In the footage, Doherty was heard saying the vulnerable woman should be sent to prison

final_reason: Intra-batch topic duplicate — same story kept from stronger source.

title: Man in his 80s dies in Tameside house fire

input.lead: The elderly man was found unresponsive at the scene of the blaze by emergency services
input.summary: The elderly man was found unresponsive at the scene of the blaze by emergency services
input.evidence_text: A man in his 80s has died in a house fire in Greater Manchester The man was found unresponsive by emergency services, who were called to the house in Foxwood Drive, Mossley, Tameside, at about 19:30 BST on Monday. He died at the scene, police confirmed A cordon remains in place and inquiries are ongoing, the force added A Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) spokesperson said five fire engines had attended the blaze "Crews arrived quickly and used four hose reels and five breathing apparatus to extinguish the fire," they said "Firefighters worked alongside colleagues from Greater Manchester Police and North West Ambulance Service and were in attendance for six hours."

title: Grieving son fights Hattersley school absence ruling 'injustice'

input.lead: Callum Dickson says he cannot understand why the request has been turned down by his sons' school
input.summary: Callum Dickson says he cannot understand why the request has been turned down by his sons' school
input.evidence_text: A dad of two has said he cannot understand why his children's school has refused to grant authorised leave for them to visit their grandfather's grave after he died while on holiday in Greece Callum Dickson, from Hyde, Greater Manchester, said they wanted to go to Halkidiki to support his mum, who remains there after her husband died from a heart attack on 26 May The 77-year-old was buried in Greece within five days of his death for religious reasons and so, while his family were unable to attend his funeral, they wanted to be able to pay their respects Pinfold Primary School in Hattersley said it did not comment publicly on individual pupils, families or specific attendance matters Callum said his father's sudden death had been difficult enough to cope with for the family, including his partner Jordon, without the added stress of facing a fine for taking their sons - aged five and six - out of school for this unauthorised absence "It just doesn't seem logical and everyone I've spoken to can't believe that was the decision they came to," he said

final_reason: Intra-batch topic duplicate — same story kept from stronger source.

title: England over-75s to face Germany for 1966 World Cup anniversary match

input.lead: The team, known as the Silver Lions, are training in York ahead of the game at the end of July
input.summary: The team, known as the Silver Lions, are training in York ahead of the game at the end of July
input.evidence_text: A group of footballers who make up England's over-75s side is gearing up for a match against Germany to mark the 60th anniversary of the country's only World Cup win The team, known as the Silver Lions, have worked alongside 1966 World Cup winner Sir Geoff Hurst as part of preparations for the game on 30 July at Boundary Park, Oldham "It's normal football, 11-a-side, on a full-size pitch, same rules as always and it's exciting and enjoyable at our age to still be involved in football," said team member Johnny Molloy The 77-year-old's niece Tracy Willits, a lecturer at the University of York St John, organised the game as part of a documentary she is making "It's always fascinated me that at that age people are playing that level of sport, so I thought it would make a nice documentary," she said "I had a word with the Germans, they were up for it and now it's built up to this massive occasion and quite frankly I am very nervous about it, but I am also very excited," she adds

final_reason: Intra-batch topic duplicate — same story kept from stronger source.

title: Preston Davey murder case prompts government pledge

input.lead: Thirteen-month old Preston Davey died after months of sexual and physical abuse by Jamie Varley
input.summary: Thirteen-month old Preston Davey died after months of sexual and physical abuse by Jamie Varley
input.evidence_text: Education Secretary Bridget Phillipson has pledged to do "everything within our power" to prevent another child facing the same fate as Preston Davey The 13-month old was sexually abused and murdered by his adoptive father, who was a former high school teacher Jamie Varley, 37, was given a whole life term for inflicting "unremitting abuse" on Preston before a final, fatal, sexual assault took his life, Preston Crown Court heard last week In the Commons, Phillipson committed to rolling out new safeguards in the Children's Wellbeing and Schools Act 2026 by March Varley's partner John McGowan-Fazakerley, 32, was jailed for 25 years for allowing Varley's treatment of the child and taking part in the sexual abuse At education questions on Monday, Phillipson said she was "glad that justice has been served" as she described the two men as "evil abusers"

title: Phoenix Nights star Ted Robbins returns 10 years after collapse

input.lead: The comic "died for 20 minutes" after collapsing during a live Phoenix Nights show at Manchester Arena
input.summary: The comic "died for 20 minutes" after collapsing during a live Phoenix Nights show at Manchester Arena
input.evidence_text: Comedian Ted Robbins is returning to the stage after collapsing during a charity show more than 10 years ago Robbins fell to the floor during his solo sketch at the opening night of the Phoenix Nights Live tour at the Manchester Arena on 31 January 2015 The comedian, actor, and broadcaster - best known for playing the villainous Den Perry in Peter Kay's Phoenix Nights - will headline The Don Banks Afternoon Variety Show on 1 July at Darwen Library Theatre in Lancashire "It's a smaller audience... but hopefully with a happier outcome," he said The "traditional variety show" will have comedy, singing and a questions from the audience, the 70-year-old said The comic said he was "eternally grateful" to the off-duty cardiothoracic doctor and paramedic who were in the audience and resuscitated him after he suffered a cardiac arrest

title: West Midlands mayor says Burnham as PM could be good for region

input.lead: Richard Parker also believes Andy Burnham could turn around Labour's election fortunes in the region
input.summary: Richard Parker also believes Andy Burnham could turn around Labour's election fortunes in the region
input.evidence_text: The West Midlands mayor hopes Andy Burnham, should he become Prime Minister, would mean more powers and funding for the region Labour's Richard Parker has offered his backing to the former mayor of Greater Manchester and said: "If he is Prime Minister, and I hope he is, I look forward to working with him to continue that path towards devolution because there's no doubt we can make better decisions here i He added Burnham had a "clear understanding" of the "difference mayors make" Burnham is the favourite to occupy Number 10 after Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday and another possible challenger to lead Labour, Wes Streeting, announced he would not make a bid Saying Burnham had done brilliantly in Greater Manchester, Parker added: "I know that he'll be committed to seeing that Whitehall devolves more powers and more funding away from Westminster into our regions." Parker also said he thought Burnham's background as a regional mayor could help turn around poor results for the Labour Party at the recent local elections in the West Midlands

input.evidence_text: A group of footballers who make up England's over-75s side is gearing up for a match against Germany to mark the 60th anniversary of the country's only World Cup win The team, known as the Silver Lions, have worked alongside 1966 World Cup winner Sir Geoff Hurst as part of preparations for the game on 30 July at Boundary Park, Oldham "It's normal football, 11-a-side, on a full-size pitch, same rules as always and it's exciting and enjoyable at our age to still be involved in football," said team member Johnny Molloy The 77-year-old's niece Tracy Willits, a lecturer at the University of York St John, organised the game as part of a documentary she is making "It's always fascinated me that at that age people are playing that level of sport, so I thought it would make a nice documentary," she said "I had a word with the Germans, they were up for it and now it's built up to this massive occasion and quite frankly I am very nervous about it, but I am also very excited," she adds

Hundreds of schools have already confirmed closures today as even higher temperatures are forecast Almost 100 schools in Somerset will be closed on Wednesday and Thursday. Residents will vote for Andy Burnham's successor in a by-election on July 30, triggered by the former mayor's return to Westminster via Makerfield Councillor Craig, who was the favourite to be Labour's mayoral candidate, has worked under Mr Burnham as deputy mayor for economy, business and inclusive growth on Greater Manchester Combined Authority. The Burnage councillor is promising to 'share' Greater Manchester's success a Coun Craig said: "Greater Manchester is a special place – from the industrial revolution, the trade union and cooperative movements and the suffragettes – this place has always fought for progress. This place changed my life and I owe it everything it gave me opportunities I coul “While Westminster left places like ours behind, Greater Manchester has taken control of our own future and we’ve started building our own success. Working alongside former mayor Andy Burnham , we have achieved so much over the – bringing buses back under public control, creating "But for too many people, who work hard and do the right thing, life still feels too hard and unaffordable. As mayor I will apply a simple test: will it make life better?

title: Dozens died during heatwaves in Greater Manchester last year - see the UK areas most at risk

input.lead: Manchester weather Dozens died during heatwaves in Greater Manchester last year - see the UK areas most at risk See our interactive map of the UK areas that faced the greatest risk of heat-associated death last year Comments News Richard Ault , Bethan Dozens died in Greater Manch
input.summary: Manchester weather Dozens died during heatwaves in Greater Manchester last year - see the UK areas most at risk See our interactive map of the UK areas that faced the greatest risk of heat-associated death last year Comments News Richard Ault , Bethan Dozens died in Greater Manch
input.evidence_text: Manchester weather Dozens died during heatwaves in Greater Manchester last year - see the UK areas most at risk See our interactive map of the UK areas that faced the greatest risk of heat-associated death last year Comments News Richard Ault , Bethan Dozens died in Greater Manchester during last year's summer heatwaves, data shows - as life-saving action plans are triggered across the UK by a rare red health warning Last summer, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) observed 1,504 heat-associated deaths in England, with 41 reported in Greater Manchester. These deaths were linked to five "heat episodes" in which average temperatures reached at least 20C The total for Greater Manchester was fewer than the 57 heat-associated deaths in 2024, despite cooler temperatures that year. While that is a significant loss of life, statistical modelling had predicted that the death toll would be twice as high, estimating 3,049 potential death According to these prediction models, 1,535 lives were saved nationwide thanks to prevention measures such as heat alerts and actions taken across the health, social care, and emergency response sectors While there were 193 more deaths than during the unseasonably cool summer of 2024, it was a huge drop from the far deadlier summers of 2023 (2,295 heat-associated deaths) and 2022 (2,985)

input.summary: Manchester weather Dozens died during heatwaves in Greater Manchester last year - see the UK areas most at risk See our interactive map of the UK areas that faced the greatest risk of heat-associated death last year Comments News Richard Ault , Bethan Dozens died in Greater Manch
input.evidence_text: Manchester weather Dozens died during heatwaves in Greater Manchester last year - see the UK areas most at risk See our interactive map of the UK areas that faced the greatest risk of heat-associated death last year Comments News Richard Ault , Bethan Dozens died in Greater Manchester during last year's summer heatwaves, data shows - as life-saving action plans are triggered across the UK by a rare red health warning Last summer, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) observed 1,504 heat-associated deaths in England, with 41 reported in Greater Manchester. These deaths were linked to five "heat episodes" in which average temperatures reached at least 20C The total for Greater Manchester was fewer than the 57 heat-associated deaths in 2024, despite cooler temperatures that year. While that is a significant loss of life, statistical modelling had predicted that the death toll would be twice as high, estimating 3,049 potential death According to these prediction models, 1,535 lives were saved nationwide thanks to prevention measures such as heat alerts and actions taken across the health, social care, and emergency response sectors While there were 193 more deaths than during the unseasonably cool summer of 2024, it was a huge drop from the far deadlier summers of 2023 (2,295 heat-associated deaths) and 2022 (2,985)

The 18-year-old suffered fatal injuries when Alexandra Bordas drove her Renault Clio into her Toyota Aygo as she drove her friends home following Chester Crown Court heard how Bordas, 35, had been 'behaving in an erratic and bizarre fashion' before she decided to get into her car. title: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield by-election: What happens next with Greater Manchester mayoralty?

input.lead: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade.
input.summary: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A
input.evidence_text: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

title: Bury woman murdered her husband, 84, who had dementia

input.summary: Minshull Street Crown Court in Manchester heard that Daryl Berman, 72, stabbed her husband of 27 years in the chest at their home in Bury on 13 March She had claimed David Berman's injuries had been sustained as he fell carrying a tray with a knife into the kitchen, but on Tuesda
input.evidence_text: Minshull Street Crown Court in Manchester heard that Daryl Berman, 72, stabbed her husband of 27 years in the chest at their home in Bury on 13 March She had claimed David Berman's injuries had been sustained as he fell carrying a tray with a knife into the kitchen, but on Tuesday the jury found her guilty of murder After the case, senior crown prosecutor Sazeeda Ismail said: "The jury saw through her lies and convicted her of murder, due to the strength of the prosecution's case." Berman had "lied to paramedics, police and family members when she claimed Mr Berman had accidentally sustained his fatal injuries", she added She will be sentenced at the same court on 3 July Also speaking after the hearing, a CPS spokesperson said the victim had suffered a stab wound to his right middle finger, which is commonly associated with people trying to defend themselves from a knife attack

input.summary: Since the beginning of the year, a major review has been underway around the city’s draft Strategy for bringing empty homes back into use to help meet demand for affordable homes across the city – while also improving communities by tackling

title: Repurposed flats will help homeless Manchester families

input.lead: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families
input.summary: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families
input.evidence_text: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families The property in Greenwood Road, Woodhouse Park, has been acquired by the Council from registered housing provider Mosscare St Vincent’s as part of wider plans to boost the city’s stock of publicly-owned, quality temporary accommodation Work is due to start this summer, and be completed in December this year, to refurbish the property as 17 self-contained flats for homeless families from Wythenshawe and south Manchester All flats will be refurbished, including new kitchens and bathrooms in each case. Two new laundry rooms for shared use will also be created, and gardens to the rear will have raised bedding for residents’ use The new accommodation will see families supported by an accommodation team based on site to help them move on as quickly as possible into permanent settled tenancies The Greenwood Road initiative is the latest example of the Council’s drive to increase its temporary accommodation stock across the city to reduce the number of out-of-area placements, helping people who become homeless to stay closer to their previous homes and support networks

title: The Greater Manchester Neurodevelopmental (ND) Early Help Model - Stockport Council

input.lead: You may have heard that Greater Manchester is introducing a new way of deciding which children and young people receive an NHS autism or ADHD assessment, and how quickly they are seen.

input.evidence_text: You may have heard that Greater Manchester is introducing a new way of deciding which children and young people receive an NHS autism or ADHD assessment, and how quickly they are seen. We know this may raise questions or concerns for families In this article, we’re sharing clear and simple information about what these changes mean in Stockport, and where you can get advice and support. Some local details are still being finalised, and we’ll add updates as soon as they are confirmed. For more information, you can also We understand how hard it can be waiting for an assessment, and how difficult it can be to know where to turn for help in the meantime. That’s why we’ve introduced a new way of getting early support from the moment you first notice signs that your child or young person may be aut We know this is a lot to take in. If you’d like to find out more, ask questions, or talk things through, you’re very welcome to come along to one of our information drop-in sessions The Strategic Lead for Neurodivergence in Stockport will be attending the following drop-in sessions supported by the Child and Adolescent Mental Health Service (CAMHS), Parents and Carers Together Stockport (PACTStockport), and Stockport Parents of ADHD Children in Education (SP In-person session with CAMHS and PACTStockport on Monday 11th May 10.30am-12pm, at Alma Lodge, 149 Buxton Road, Stockport, SK2 6EL. Refreshments will be provided. Please reserve a spot for this session

title: Young musicians shine at Rochdale's Summer Sizzler music event - Rochdale Borough Council

input.lead: More than 220 gifted musicians, including children as young as 7 years old, have been impressing local audiences with their talents
input.summary: More than 220 gifted musicians, including children as young as 7 years old, have been impressing local audiences with their talents
input.evidence_text: More than 220 gifted musicians, including children as young as 7 years old, have been impressing local audiences with their talents This follows Rochdale Music Service’s annual Summer Sizzler event held at Middleton Arena, where local schools and children, who have been supported by the service, came together to perform in front of the Mayor of Rochdale and their friends and family With the sound of guitars, violins, flutes, trumpets and trombones playing in perfect harmony among many other instruments, participants played a variety of music, from traditional rock to classical ensembles, throughout the day during 3 separate concerts For some, this was their very first live musical performance as they played in small bands and a mass orchestra alongside pupils from different schools in the borough who they may otherwise have never met To tie in with the upcoming season, all of the music was summer themed, with special arrangements put together so that children, who have only just started playing and know a few notes, could still join in alongside more seasoned performers This included Rochdale Music Service’s very own playful take on ‘The 12 Days of Christmas’, which had been reworded as 'The 12 Days of Summer' so participants could count down in song to items such as 6 inflatable dolphins and 5 rubber rings

title: Innovative approach to speech and language support earns ministerial praise - Rochdale Borough Council

input.lead: A government minister has praised Rochdale borough’s award-winning approach to speech, language and communication support which has seen waiting times for families significantly reduce
input.summary: A government minister has praised Rochdale borough’s award-winning approach to speech, language and communication support which has seen waiting times for families significantly reduce
input.evidence_text: A government minister has praised Rochdale borough’s award-winning approach to speech, language and communication support which has seen waiting times for families significantly reduce This recognition came as part of a visit by the Minister of State for School Standards, Georgia Gould, who learned about the innovative new methods that have been introduced locally to help families, schools and early years settings to address children’s communication needs earli Children referred for speech and language therapy previously waited up to 44 weeks for a specialist appointment due to high levels of need in the borough However, local evidence found that in many cases children could often be supported sooner if families and professionals had access to practical tools and guidance at home or in settings like the classroom In response, Rochdale Borough Council and its NHS partners have introduced a new system of early intervention, which enables families and professionals to access instant speech and language support at the touch of a button This is thanks to things like artifical intelligence (AI) technology, tailored speech and language advice through WhatsApp, a coaching platform called Help at Hand, and an online virtual noticeboard which offers practical tips, resources and activities

title: Bolton Library marks centenary of the 1926 General Strike - Bolton Council

input.lead: Bolton Library and Museum is marking the centenary of one of the largest industrial disputes in British history with a special display
input.summary: Bolton Library and Museum is marking the centenary of one of the largest industrial disputes in British history with a special display
input.evidence_text: Bolton Library and Museum is marking the centenary of one of the largest industrial disputes in British history with a special display 100 years ago, in May 1926, millions of workers across Britain took part in the General Strike The strike originated as a dispute between coal mine owners and miners over reduced wages and longer working hours When the Trades Union Congress (TUC) declared their support for the miners, other industrial workers - including those in transport, engineering, and textiles - also walked out, and it became the first ever General Strike in British history The strike was called on 3 May 1926 and lasted nine days, where the first few days of the strike saw the transport network in Bolton grind to a halt without bus and train drivers Food deliveries were delayed, and some textile mills stopped operating

title: Bury Council sets out next steps for Six Town Housing Limited

input.summary: Press release — 23 June 2026 11:45 Share Share this story Copy link Link copied! Download Bury Council is moving forward with its plan to formally close Six Town Housing Limited (STH), the organisation previously responsible for managing much of the borough’s social housing In Fe
input.evidence_text: Press release — 23 June 2026 11:45 Share Share this story Copy link Link copied! Download Bury Council is moving forward with its plan to formally close Six Town Housing Limited (STH), the organisation previously responsible for managing much of the borough’s social housing In February 2024, the council brought most of the 7,800 properties back under its direct management, but STH continued to own and manage 117 specialist social rent properties Last September the council decided to begin the process to close STH and secure a long-term future for these remaining homes The council has been undertaking detailed work to review the future of the STH portfolio. This has included an in-depth financial analysis which concluded that bringing these homes back into direct council ownership might not be the best viable option in the long term No decision has been made. The council is keeping all options open and is exploring the potential for these homes to be transferred to another Registered Provider of Social Housing (a housing association) as one possible way to ensure services continue to be delivered in a stable

input.evidence_text: Wigan Council has submitted a planning application for a major refurbishment of Leigh Market, including proposals for a new roof, entrance, and lobby area Aware of the significant connection that the market has to the people of Leigh, the local authority included the building’s revamp as part of its Levelling Up (now Local Regeneration Fund) scheme As the planning application moves forward, preparatory works are also set to take place at several units within Spinning Gate Shopping Centre. Following discussions with traders, the market will be temporarily located to these units next year whilst the refurbishment of the main Councillor Dane Anderton, portfolio holder for economic development and regeneration, said: “The proposals for Leigh Market represent an exciting opportunity to refurbish a much-loved community venue that is home to a diverse group of traders “While these plans aim to protect the future of the market and put it in a much more stable condition, we want to ensure that the development works for both traders and shoppers alike. I’m pleased that after productive discussions with traders, the temporary market will be locate Boasting a long and proud market history, Leigh is believed to have held an outdoor market for hundreds of years. title: Health column: Smoke Free Generation - A rare landmark moment for public health

input.lead: It may have become a little lost amid major political and global events but there was a landmark moment for public health last month, the significance of which should not be overlooked On 29th April the government’s Tobacco and Vapes Bill became law, making it illegal to sell tob
input.summary: It may have become a little lost amid major political and global events but there was a landmark moment for public health last month, the significance of which should not be overlooked On 29th April the government’s Tobacco and Vapes Bill became law, making it illegal to sell tob
input.evidence_text: It may have become a little lost amid major political and global events but there was a landmark moment for public health last month, the significance of which should not be overlooked On 29th April the government’s Tobacco and Vapes Bill became law, making it illegal to sell tobacco to anyone born on, or after, 1st January 2009 - and, in doing so, creating the UK’s first ‘smoke‑free generation’ Make no mistake that this is one of the most significant public health reforms in a generation, and one that sends a clear signal: preventing harm before it begins matters Smoking remains the leading preventable cause of illness and early death in the UK. It significantly increases a person’s risk of cancer, heart disease and stroke, places huge pressure on the NHS, and continues to widen the gap in life expectancy across our communities The new law takes a bold, long‑term approach to breaking that cycle by ensuring today’s children never legally start smoking at all We know that information, advice and individual willpower can only go so far. Real, lasting change happens when policy and environments support people to make healthier choices

These have included Holocaust survivors, ‘witches’ and Victorian factory workers. She likes to link these stories to her own experiences, such as fostering refugees and Accessibility is at the heart of everything Sally creates. title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

Matthew Bourne’s award-winning The Car Man is back! Loosely based on Bizet’s ever-popular opera, The Car Man has one of the most thrilling and instantly recognisable scores in New Adventures’ repertoire The familiar 19th Century Spanish cigarette factory becomes a greasy 1950’s garage-diner in the American Mid-West where the dreams and passions of a small-town are shattered by the arrival of a handsome and enigmatic stranger. Fuelled by heat and desire, the inhabitants are drive Matthew Bourne’s vivid storytelling combines with one of the most passionately dramatic scores ever written, with music by Terry Davies featuring Rodion Shchedrin’s Carmen Suite (after Bizet’s Carmen), to create a dance thriller like no other The Car Man will ignite your senses and leave you breathless ★★★★★ "Buckle up for the ride of your life. Their rowdy retro sound was deeply influenced by vintage soul, R&B and gospel traditions, Van Morrison and the Band. A formidable live unit with a reputation for stage-quaking shows, they signed Nathaniel Rateliff & the Night Sweats’ self-titled debut album was issued in August 2015, preceded by appearances at the Newport Folk Festival and The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon. input.summary: Mel Bonis Ophélie Op 165 Emily Dunbar New work* Sergei Rachmaninov Rhapsody on a Theme of Paganini Op 43 Piotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky Symphony No 6 in B minor Op 74 ‘Pathétique’ Clemens Schuldt, Jasper Lecon* conductors Liana Storey piano RNCM Symphony Orchestra Tchaikovsky’s ‘ Pathé
input.evidence_text: Mel Bonis Ophélie Op 165 Emily Dunbar New work* Sergei Rachmaninov Rhapsody on a Theme of Paganini Op 43 Piotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky Symphony No 6 in B minor Op 74 ‘Pathétique’ Clemens Schuldt, Jasper Lecon* conductors Liana Storey piano RNCM Symphony Orchestra Tchaikovsky’s ‘ Pathétique ’ Symphony stands as an unnerving prelude to the composer’s untimely death, which occurred just nine days after its premiere. Bleak yet epic, the true narrative behind this programmatic work is shrouded in as much mystery as the circumstances surroundin Tchaikovsky sets the tone with a haunting opening, before moving into a lilting yet unsettled waltz. The third movement creates the illusion of a triumphant conclusion, only for this hope to be undone in the final movement Perhaps a requiem to himself, the final Adagio lamentoso strays from the traditional symphonic form. Is this sombre, mournful elegy a resignation to fate?

input.evidence_text: The £35 million project set within 154 acres has transformed the former site of Worsley New Hall, restoring the historic walled garden, lake and surviving buildings Originally built for the 1st Earl of Ellesmere between 1840 and 1845, Worsley New Hall was a grand Gothic-style mansion, complete with glorious, formal landscaped gardens It’s now one of UK’s must-see gardens with so much to enjoy for visitors of all ages Step inside the magnificent 11-acre Weston Walled Garden, the site’s jewel in the crown. One of the largest Victorian walled gardens in the UK, it showcases a range of growing techniques and edible plants There’s also the spectacular Paradise Garden showcasing exotic planting inspired by Asiatic and Mediterranean gardens. The orchard and nearby bee and butterfly garden, have been designed to attract wildlife The Peel Learning Garden, supported by The Peel Group, will help schoolchildren learn how plants affect our lives and the planet, whilst the Community Wellbeing Garden provides a space for people to garden, socialise and relax

input.evidence_text: One of the things that struck me listening to Alan Milburn's interim review on young people and work was his acknowledgement that employers, particularly SMEs, will need more support if they are expected to play a bigger role in helping young people transition into employment At almost exactly the same time, the government announced further changes to T Level industry placements, relaxing some of the original requirements after years of challenges securing enough employer participation. The move follows a significant decline in placements, with FE Wee To me, those two things are closely connected, for years, the conversation around skills and early careers has focused heavily on supply. More qualifications, more bootcamps, more people trained, more pathways into industry Those things matter. But creating talent is only half the equation. The other half is making sure employers have the capacity, confidence and support to absorb that talent Over the last year, Manchester Digital has been running TConnect, a pilot supported by the Gatsby Foundation and Greater Manchester Combined Authority that aimed to make it easier for employers to engage with T Level placements. Like most pilots, it raised as many questions as it One thing became clear very quickly. The challenge is rarely convincing employers that workplace experience matters, most employers already believe that. The challenge is creating opportunities in a way that works alongside the realities of running a business. We saw employers co

It was emotionally unguarded in her music, and uncompromising in public – used to speak uncomfortable truths even when doing so would bring personal and professional damage. The Surge: An Ode to Sinéad O’Connor takes Conceived, choreographed and directed by the Tony Award-winning Sonya Tayeh, The Surge is the first major stage production devoted to O’Connor’s life and work, presented by New York’s Joyce Theater and Factory International . Performed by a company of 10 women spanning a wide ran “Sinéad has been a part of my life since her first album arrived blaring out car windows in Detroit, where I’m from,” Sonya Tayeh told Crack Magazine . “All of those memories came flooding back when she passed. I was heartbroken and had this sudden vision of 10 women standing in That initial image grew into a deliberate challenge to how age is typically framed in the dance world. Такие события совмещают атмосферу клубного мероприятия, речной прогулки и обзорной экскурсии по городу. title: Андрей Кравченко

input.lead: Андрей Кравченко at Brixton Jamm
input.summary: Андрей Кравченко в Лондоне! 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне
input.evidence_text: 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне Андрей Кравченко покорил сердца тысяч слушателей благодаря своему уникальному голосу, харизме и способности превращать личные истории в песни, близкие каждому. В его творчестве смешиваются романтическая лирика, современное эстрадное звучание и проникновенная искренность После серии аншлаговых выступлений на самых престижных сценах Украины, среди которых Жовтневый Дворец и Дворец “Украина”, певец продолжает свое концертное путешествие и приглашает поклонников на особую встречу в Лондоне Концерты Андрея Кравченко известны своей теплой атмосферой: здесь нет дистанции между сценой и залом, ведь каждое выступление превращается в откровенный разговор на языке музыки. Гостей ждут любимые хиты, живой звук, море эмоций и моменты, которые захочется сохранить в памяти над Важной частью творческой миссии артиста остается поддержка Украины — часть средств от концерта будет передана на помощь ВСУ! Подарите себе вечер настоящей романтики, красивой музыки и искренних чувств вместе с Андреем Кравченко

​We're heading back to Lock 91 on the 24th June and making the most of it's fantastic canal side terrace for an evening of relaxed networking with people from across Manchester's tech scene

title: Innovation Office Hours with Naomi Timperley - for founders, startups, scale-ups, innovation leaders and ambitious businesses looking to grow
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

Overview

Join Naomi for a 30-minute one-to-one for founders, startups, scale-ups, innovation leaders and ambitious businesses looking to grow.

Expert Office Hours - for founders, startups, scale-ups, innovation leaders and ambitious businesses looking to grow

Sign up for a 30-minute one-to-one at The Tech Hub at No.1 Circle Square hosted by Bruntwood SciTech.

Expert Office Hours with Naomi Timperley

Building a business can be exciting, but it can also be lonely. Sometimes what you need isn't another webinar or workshop, you need someone independent to help you sense-check your thinking, challenge assumptions, unlock opportunities and identify the next steps.

Join Naomi Timperley for a free 30-minute one-to-one session.

Naomi has spent more than 15 years supporting founders, startups, scale-ups and innovation-led organisations. She is Co-Founder of Tech North Advocates, UK Lead for Global Tech Advocates, Co-Founder of End Game and was named Computer Weekly's Most Influential Woman in UK Tech 2025.

Whether you're looking to:

• Validate a new idea or business model
• Navigate growth challenges
• Explore AI adoption and innovation opportunities
• Build strategic partnerships
• Improve your proposition and positioning
• Access funding, investment or support programmes
• Expand your network and connections
• Sense-check a key business decision
• Work out what comes next

These informal office hours are an opportunity to discuss your challenges, opportunities and ambitions and leave with practical advice, useful connections and clear next steps.

Suitable for founders, startups, scale-ups, innovation leaders and ambitious businesses looking to grow.

Use the booking form to book a slot. Time slots will be for 30 minutes.

See you there!

Manchester’s tech and investment scene is firing on all cylinders, but what happens when we look beyond our own backyard to drive the next wave of growth?


This Thursday Gathering with Sister explores what happens when you take the exciting momentum in Greater Manchester and link up with international partners. With a spotlight on connecting Ireland and Manchester, we’ll be discussing how to turn the growing ties between these two thriving ecosystems into the next big opportunity for local businesses.\xa0


Whether you're a founder just starting to build, an investor looking for the next breakout, or a business leader who hasn’t thought about international growth yet, this session is for you.


You’ll hear from experts from both sides of the Irish sea, including Invest Manchester, Innovation Exchange Ireland, Uniquely, Guinness Enterprise Centre, ClimbUK and Platform94 on how regional and cross-border partnerships and shared talent can fast-track your progress.\xa0

title: Bioengineering Pathways: Academia and Entrepreneurship
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

The session will highlight how scientists can navigate careers spanning academia, industry, and entrepreneurship. Drawing on the experiences of our speakers, we will explore complementary career trajectories, including:

  • ​Transitioning across international biotech ecosystems and startup environments

  • ​Combining academic leadership with company co-founding

  • ​Bridging fundamental research with commercial application, particularly in sustainability and climate biotech

​Whether you are exploring commercialisation, developing new technologies, or considering future career directions, join us to hear inspiring stories and learn how Nucleate UK partners with Advanced Research and Invention Agency (ARIA) to accelerate ambitious ideas.

Speakers:
Lucia Burdisso, PhD - Lucia is the Chief Scientific Officer at Deep Blue Biotech, where she leads the development of a cyanobacterial synthetic biology platform for sustainable, CO₂-based chemical production. A biotechnologist with a PhD and more than 10 years of experience in R&D across academia and biotech startups, Lucia has worked in 10 laboratories across 4 countries, including startups in Argentina and the San Francisco Bay Area, and is now based in Sheffield.

Ressa Lebedinec, PhD - Ressa is a molecular mycologist and Senior Biomolecular Engineer at The University of Manchester Biotechnology Institute, with over 10 years of experience in fungal biology, biomolecular engineering, and sustainable biotechnology. She is the co-founder of mushroom+, a deep-tech platform formed in 2025 within the Carbon13 venture builder ecosystem.
mushroom+ is developing technologies that enhance the protein content and performance of mushrooms, transforming existing mushroom farms into climate-positive protein hubs. By increasing protein yield and nutritional density per kilogram of biomass, the company aims to address critical environmental and economic challenges in food production, without requiring the food system to be rebuilt from scratch.

Daniele Parisi, PhD - Daniele is a Senior Scientist at C3, where he leads efforts in scaling up fermentation processes and solving challenges related to circular biomanufacturing. With over a decade of experience in sustainability-driven research and development, he brings deep expertise in fermentation, downstream processing (DSP), and biocatalysis. Daniele holds a PhD in Molecular Biology and Biochemistry, and has contributed to both early-stage sustainability-focused startups and global companies, including his recent work with Croda.

Drinks and mexican food will be provided during the networking session with peers and potential collaborators.

title: CONEXEN Manchester July
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

Registration needed: https:/conexen.net/conexen-events/conexen-manchester-july/

Welcome to CONEXEN Manchester – A warm and welcoming community for professionals and founders in biopharma, life sciences, and health tech in Manchester! Here, we invite you to join us for an informal and friendly gathering where connections are made, knowledge is shared, and friendships are formed. Come with an open heart and a willingness to “listen” rather than “sell.”

​​We will be hosting our Manchester event at the amazing space of Cloud 23 Cocktail Bar. It is a casual setting where we can come together, exchange greetings, engage in conversation, and simply enjoy a pleasant time.

​​Picture this as more than just an event, it’s a life sciences community gathering. Our focus is on providing you with an opportunity to meet fellow professionals and founders in a relaxed and amiable environment. We’ll share conversations over delicious food and drinks, and leave with a sense of excitement and a pocketful of new contacts and ideas.

​​Join us for an evening surrounded by incredible people, and let’s make it a night to remember. Looking forward to sharing good company and creating memorable connections!

​Theme: The Forces Reshaping Work & Culture in Life Sciences.

Join a focused discussion led by Paul Bradley-Law on the forces redefining culture in a hybrid world. Our panellists will explore how hybrid working, evolving HR trends, leadership style, and a multi-generational workforce are reshaping how organisations operate, alongside the growing influence of AI in the workplace.

We’ll examine how trust is built through clarity and candour in uncertain times—without defaulting to micromanagement—and how to address “proximity bias” to ensure fair visibility, recognition, and opportunity across distributed teams.

Join peers shaping the North-West Life Sciences ecosystem and driving the region’s growing influence.

​Moderator: Paul Bradley-Law (Founder, The Great People Machine)

​Panelists:

  • Ike Cooke, Business Growth & Commercialisation Manager, LYVA Labs

  • Julia Buckler, Senior Director, Site Head, Qiagen

​The format of the night:

  • ​18:00 Doors open – drinks, networking and meeting great people.

  • ​18:30 Talks start

  • ​19:15 Drinks, food and networking.

  • ​20:30 End of the night – you leave with new contacts and fresh ideas.

​​Venue: Cloud 23 Cocktail Bar – The Manchester Deansgate Hotel, 303 Deansgate, Manchester M3 4LQ, United Kingdom.

This event is proudly supported by Nexa Life Sciences and Kadans Science Partner, hosted by Kadans Science Partner.

​

​And finally…

​This is a ticketed event. Food and drinks for the night are included.

​Please let your colleagues and industry friends know about us, we hope to build it into a great resource for the ecosystem over the coming months and years. Attendance will be taken at this event. If you register but don’t show up, this has a knock-on effect on event arrangements. If it happens 3 times, we may need to reconsider your participation. This is simply a measure to allow appropriate respect to the organisers, speakers, sponsors, and your fellow Conexers.

​See you there!

​Ashley, Julia, and Sajid
Manchester Organisers

​Tickets FAQs: https:/www.conexen.net/faq

Questions

title: Unlocking New Possibilities with the Oracle AI Ecosystem
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

An executive innovation experience, connecting AI, data and applications to real business outcomes.

Unlocking New Possibilities with the Oracle AI Ecosystem

Join us at Oracle's offices in Moorgate, London for Kovaion Connect 2026 - a focused executive briefing showing how Oracle Applications, Data, AI, OCI and Oracle APEX come together to help organisations innovate faster, operate smarter and unlock measurable business outcomes.

This is a half-day session built for senior decision-makers, combining thought leadership, the latest Oracle AI product updates, live demonstrations and one-to-one advisory conversations. The morning briefing moves from strategy to live innovation, followed by a networking lunch and optional private advisory slots in the afternoon. One Oracle. More value. Limitless innovation.

What you can expect

  • Executive insights into how AI, data and enterprise applications are reshaping the way organisations operate

  • The newest embedded AI capabilities across Oracle Fusion Applications

  • Release highlights and AI enhancements in Oracle APEX that accelerate enterprise innovation

  • Live demos of intelligent enterprise applications built on Oracle APEX and Oracle AI

  • A practical view of building an AI-ready data foundation with unified analytics

  • Private 1:1 executive advisory discussions with Oracle and Kovaion experts (afternoon, by request)

  • A networking lunch to exchange perspectives with peers and specialists

The day at a glance

  • 09:30 – 13:30 - Main session and networking lunch

  • 13:30 – 17:00 - Optional 1:1 executive advisory sessions

Who should attend

This briefing is designed for CIOs and CTOs, Heads of Applications, IT and Operations, Data and AI leaders, and Transformation heads - the people setting the enterprise technology, AI and digital strategy agenda.

Featured speakers include

  • Simon Hunt - Head of Product Management, Oracle APEX, Oracle UK

  • Mohana Priya Venkatachalam - Chief Projects Officer, Kovaion UK

  • Srinivas Nadella - Strategic Accounts Manager, Kovaion UK

With more Oracle and industry speakers to be announced.

You can view full agenda here - https:/www.kovaion.com/kovaion-connect-2026/

Venue: Oracle Corporation UK Ltd., One South Place, London EC2M 2RB - minutes from Moorgate, Liverpool Street and Bank stations, with rail, Underground and Elizabeth line connections.

Abstract;
In this age of AI, where AI permeates our every system. Do we need to think differently about AI. Do we need to move beyond what it CAN do to what it SHOULD do and HOW and for WHOM?

Speaker:
Stuart Coulson is the Principal Cyber Security Service Manager at a global telecoms giant, the founder of HiddenText, and someone who’s spent 20+ years turning complexity into clarity. He doesn’t do fluff. Whether mentoring start-ups or advising boardrooms, he challenges lazy thinking, strips away jargon, and leaves people with ideas they can actually use. Recognised as Cyber Mentor of the Year (2023) and a Top 50 Tech Leader (2025), Stuart’s mission is simple: build resilience, grow leaders, and make mentoring a culture that outlives him.
